title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses a low risk, but improper use or reliance on it without validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives/negatives. Additionally, if the tool is compromised or maliciously modified, it could introduce security risks.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure its reliability and accuracy before deploying it in production.
Monitor the tool's source code repository for updates or potential compromises, especially since it is open-source.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside other established vulnerability management processes.

title: shell-quote quote() does not escape newlines in object .op values
description:
### Summary

`shell-quote`'s `quote()` function did not validate object-token inputs against the operator model used by `parse()`. The `.op` field was backslash-escaped character by character using `/(.)/g`, which in JavaScript does not match line terminators (`\n`, `\r`, U+2028, U+2029). A line terminator in `.op` therefore passed through unescaped into the output; POSIX shells treat a literal `\n` as a command separator, so any content after it would execute as a second command.

The vulnerabl

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-9277.

title: The User Frontend: AI Powered Frontend Posting, User Directory, Profile, Membership & User...
description: The User Frontend: AI Powered Frontend Posting, User Directory, Profile, Membership & User Registration plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to unauthorized modification of data due to a missing capability check on the user_subscription_cancel() function in all versions up to, and including, 4.3.2. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to cancel any user's subscription pack, including administrators.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with minimal privileges (Subscriber-level access) to cancel any user's subscription pack, including those of administrators. This can lead to disruption of services, loss of revenue, and potential reputational damage for organizations relying on subscription-based models.
recommended_actions: Update the 'The User Frontend' plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available or disable the plugin temporarily if no fix is available.
Restrict access to Subscriber-level accounts by reviewing and limiting user roles and permissions to minimize exposure.
Monitor subscription-related activities and logs to detect any unauthorized modifications or cancellations.

title: A vulnerability has been identified in SINEC INS (All versions < V1.0 SP2 Update 6). The...
description: A vulnerability has been identified in SINEC INS (All versions < V1.0 SP2 Update 6). The application does not properly sanitize user input in the /api/sftp/uploadFiles endpoint, allowing the injection of shell command payloads via crafted directory names. These payloads are stored and executed when directory listings are retrieved. This could allow an authenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary commands on the underlying operating system with the privileges of the affected service user (s

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands on the underlying operating system, potentially leading to data breaches, service disruptions, or lateral movement within the network.
recommended_actions: Immediately update SINEC INS to V1.0 SP2 Update 6 or later to patch the vulnerability.
Restrict access to the affected endpoint (/api/sftp/uploadFiles) and monitor for unusual activity in directory listings.
Implement input validation and sanitization mechanisms to prevent command injection vulnerabilities in future deployments.

title: A vulnerability has been identified in SINEC INS (All versions < V1.0 SP2 Update 6). The affected...
description: A vulnerability has been identified in SINEC INS (All versions < V1.0 SP2 Update 6). The affected application uses a password hashing implementation with a static, hardcoded salt shared across all users and installations, and is configured with an insufficient number of iterations. This could allow an attacker to efficiently recover user passwords using brute-force or precomputed attacks, potentially resulting in unauthorized access.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to recover user passwords through brute-force or precomputed attacks due to weak password hashing implementation. This could lead to unauthorized access, data breaches, and potential compromise of critical systems.
recommended_actions: Update SINEC INS to version V1.0 SP2 Update 6 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Implement strong password policies and enforce multi-factor authentication (MFA) to reduce the risk of unauthorized access.
Conduct a security audit to identify and address any additional vulnerabilities or misconfigurations in the affected systems.

The affected application does not properly sanitize path input in the `GET /api/sftp/uploadFiles` endpoint used for directory listing. This allows path traversal through crafted input, enabling access to unintended file system locations.

description: A path traversal vulnerability was found in awxkit, the CLI tool for AWX. The YAML !include directive does not sanitize file paths, allowing an attacker to craft a malicious YAML file that reads arbitrary YAML-formatted files from the local filesystem when a user imports it using "awx --conf.format yaml import". This is a client-side vulnerability requiring user interaction.

title: SQL injection in the ‘two_steps_auth_code’ parameter processed by the ‘twoStepsAuthVerification’...
description: SQL injection in the ‘two_steps_auth_code’ parameter processed by the ‘twoStepsAuthVerification’ function within the ‘/user-login’ endpoint. The two-factor authentication (2FA) functionality can be accessed without prior authentication, allowing unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries on the backend database. A successful exploit could lead to database enumeration, the unauthorised creation of privileged users, the modification or deletion of critical information, and denial-o

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in the 'two_steps_auth_code' parameter allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries, potentially leading to full database compromise, creation of privileged users, data exfiltration, and denial of service. This could result in severe data breaches, financial losses, and reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Immediately sanitize and validate all user inputs, especially the 'two_steps_auth_code' parameter, to prevent SQL injection.
Implement parameterized queries or prepared statements in the 'twoStepsAuthVerification' function to mitigate SQL injection risks.
Conduct a comprehensive security audit of the application, focusing on authentication and input validation mechanisms.

title: The MailerPress – Email Marketing, Newsletter, Email Automation & WooCommerce Emails plugin for...
description: The MailerPress – Email Marketing, Newsletter, Email Automation & WooCommerce Emails plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via Campaign HTML Content Field in all versions up to, and including, 2.0.4 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with author-level access and above, to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page. The public-facing campa

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with author-level access or higher to inject malicious scripts into campaign pages. These scripts can execute whenever a user accesses the injected page, potentially leading to data theft, session hijacking, or further exploitation of the affected site.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Update the MailerPress plugin to a patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Restrict author-level access to trusted users only and review user permissions.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block malicious scripts.

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

io_uring/waitid: clear...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

io_uring/waitid: clear waitid info before copying it to userspace

IORING_OP_WAITID stores its result fields in struct io_waitid::info and
later copies them to userspace siginfo. The prep path initializes the
request arguments, but it does not initialize info itself.

If the wait operation completes without reporting a child event, the common
wait code can return without writing wo_info. In that case io_waitid_finish()
still co

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability in the Linux kernel's io_uring subsystem could potentially allow uninitialized memory to be exposed to userspace. This may lead to information disclosure, which could be leveraged by attackers to obtain sensitive data or escalate privileges.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest security patches and updates to the Linux kernel to address CVE-2026-46315.
Audit systems using io_uring to identify potential exposure to this vulnerability and monitor for unusual activity.
Implement additional monitoring and logging to detect any exploitation attempts targeting this vulnerability.

title: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Disclosure in...
description: The Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Sensitive Information Disclosure in versions up to and including 7.0.10. This is due to three compounding design flaws: (1) the plugin leaks a valid backend AJAX nonce (revslider_actions) to all authenticated users including Subscribers via the admin_footer hook; (2) the wordpress.create.image_from_url action is explicitly allowlisted in the $user_allowed array, bypassing the administrator-only access control; (3) the create_wordpress_i

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Slider Revolution plugin for WordPress could allow sensitive information disclosure, potentially exposing backend AJAX nonces and bypassing access controls. This could lead to unauthorized access, data leakage, or further exploitation of the WordPress site.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the Slider Revolution plugin to a version higher than 7.0.10 to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and restrict user roles and permissions to minimize exposure to sensitive actions.
Monitor server logs for any suspicious activity related to the revslider_actions or wordpress.create.image_from_url endpoints.

title:

This issue...

Improper Neutralization of Alternate XSS Syntax vulnerability in Apache Answer.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 2.0.1, which fixes the issue.

title: The Apache Airflow Samba provider's `GCSToSambaOperator` joined GCS object names to the SMB...
description: The Apache Airflow Samba provider's `GCSToSambaOperator` joined GCS object names to the SMB destination path without a containment check, so an object named with `../` segments resolved a write path outside the configured `destination_path`. An attacker able to write objects into the source GCS bucket — typically an external data producer distinct from the trusted DAG author — could write files to arbitrary locations on the Samba target when the operator ran. Upgrade apache-airflow-providers-sam

title: CVE-2026-10566 - A weakness has been identified in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.2. This affects the function Me
description: A weakness has been identified in FoundationAgents MetaGPT up to 0.8.2. This affects the function Message.check_instruct_content of the file metagpt/schema.py. Executing a manipulation of the argument mapping can lead to deserialization. The attack is restricted to local execution. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in MetaGPT allows for deserialization attacks through the manipulation of the argument mapping in the Message.check_instruct_content function. This could lead to arbitrary code execution or other malicious actions if exploited. However, the attack is limited to local execution, reducing its overall risk compared to remote vulnerabilities.
recommended_actions: Immediately update to a patched version of MetaGPT once it becomes available. Monitor the project's repository or announcements for updates.
Restrict local access to systems running vulnerable versions of MetaGPT to trusted personnel only, and ensure proper access controls are in place.
Conduct a thorough review of your systems to identify any potential misuse of the vulnerability and monitor for suspicious activity.

title: CVE-2026-10565 - A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. The impacted element is the function gmm
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Open5GS up to 2.7.6. The impacted element is the function gmm_state_security_mode of the file src/amf/gmm-sm.c of the component NGAP Handover. Performing a manipulation results in race condition. The attack can be initiated remotely. The complexity of an attack is rather high. The exploitability is regarded as difficult. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The pull request to fix this issue awaits acceptance.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS could lead to race conditions during NGAP handover processes, potentially causing service disruptions or unauthorized access in 5G networks. However, the attack complexity is high, limiting the likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Open5GS to the latest version once the patch is released and approved.
Monitor network traffic for unusual activity or signs of exploitation targeting NGAP handover processes.
Implement additional security measures such as rate limiting or sandboxing to mitigate potential exploitation.

description: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in GeniexWebView component in Transsion AI Assistant Lifestyle application (com.transsion.aiassistantlifestyle) all versions on Android allows remote attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the WebView context via crafted web_action_data URL parameter.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10510.

title: CVE-2026-10100 - The Simple Custom Login Page plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via t
description: The Simple Custom Login Page plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via the color settings fields (Page Background, Form Background, Text Color, Link Color) in versions up to and including 1.0.3. This is due to insufficient input sanitization of the color option values (they were registered with register_setting() and stored via the Settings API/update_option() with no sanitize_callback) combined with the values being output into a <style> block on wp-login.php using esc_attr(), which is incorrect for a CSS context (it does not escape ;, {, }, / or *). This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with administrator-level access and above, to inject arbitrary CSS rules into the login page that are rendered for all unauthenticated visitors, enabling UI-redress and credential-phishing attacks.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-10100.
